detectors.periodic_channel_sum_MR: fault changes channel sum vs clean by > 0.05

emergence_summary.honest_verdict: comparable-but-richer-and-messier than the constructed version: 5 of 8 realistic faults are detected, and their by-class localization EMERGES as a 2x2 partition by (changes channel integral) x (breaks translation equivariance) -- conservation-only=['channel_swap', 'global_renorm'], translation-only=['gaussian_noise'], both=['boundary_band_corrupt', 'region_dropout'] -- rather than a clean 1:1 diagonal. The 3 undetected faults ['mode_truncation', 'sharpen', 'spatial_shift'] are the transport/high-frequency bugs, which are structurally invisible to both MRs; on the SUTs where they reach realistic magnitude (['sharpen', 'spatial_shift']) they STILL escape, so this blind region is real, not a construction artifact.

honesty_boundary: K=6 trained FNO-2D checkpoints over FD Burgers/heat, one realistic mechanism-level fault catalogue at recorded magnitudes. Bounded by-class emergence evidence; faults are NOT tailored to the MRs and thresholds are unchanged. NOT a real-world fault-detection rate, reliability, baseline-superiority, or cross-architecture generalization claim. On these smooth low-resolution fields some transport/high-frequency faults reach only small realistic magnitudes; this is reported, not hidden.
